meaning: adj.摆动的,不稳定的,(字迹等)歪斜的
examples: Corporate lending and parts of consumer credit, such as credit cards and car loans, are wobbly.
That's true for voters. But government subsidy is a wobbly foundation on which to build a business.
The evening got off to a wobbly start.
An optical laser reads the transparency and creates a wobbly, out-of-tune, almost eerie-sounding keyboard effect.
Talk now of limits and freezes, is talk of a wobbly target pinned to a blurred picture.
